commonIssues:
Many households claim their BPL (Below Poverty Line) cards are being wrongly converted to APL (Above Poverty Line) status due to technical glitches or erroneous data, causing them to lose benefits.

How to fix:

  • Visit the nearest Grama One, Karnataka One, or Seva Sindhu center with your original income certificate to prove your BPL eligibility.
  • File an online grievance on the e-Janashakthi portal or contact the Food Inspector of your taluk with supporting documents for re-conversion.

A major issue for laborers, construction workers, and senior citizens, whose fingerprints are not recognized by machines due to worn-out fingerprints, preventing them from receiving rations.

How to fix:

  • Update your biometric details at the nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra. The new biometrics will sync with the PDS system.
  • For senior citizens or exceptional cases, the state allows iris scanning or an OTP-based authentication using the Aadhaar-linked mobile number as an alternative. Request the fair price shop dealer to switch modes.

Households are facing cancellations because of outdated income limits (fixed in the mid-2010s) that do not account for inflation, or because the cumulative income of separate family members is calculated together.

How to fix:

  • Procure a freshly updated, accurate Income Certificate from the Tahsildar via the Nadakacheri portal.
  • Submit a formal petition to the local Food Inspector alongside the new Income Certificate to manually override the outdated demographic data holding back your BPL privileges.

Issues with Aadhaar not being properly linked to the PDS database prevent beneficiaries from receiving ration, often leading to "No Aadhaar, No Ration" situations on the ground.

How to fix:

  • Visit ahara.kar.nic.in to verify the Aadhaar linking status of all family members on your ration card.
  • If unlinked, personally submit physical copies of your ration card and unlinked family members' Aadhaar cards to your local FPS dealer or Gram Panchayat for manual forced linking in the e-POS machine.

The state has periodically paused the acceptance of new BPL card applications, leading to difficulties for new families or those whose needs have changed, such as farmers needing cards for other government schemes.

How to fix:

  • While the portal frequently shuts down general applications, the state often opens temporary "windows" based on urgency. Keep track of press releases from the Food & Civil Supplies Department.
  • In medical or extreme emergency cases, a direct petition endorsed by an MLA or Tahsildar to the district Joint Director of Food and Civil Supplies can expedite a manual review.

The state is currently verifying over 7.7 lakh "suspected" cards, which has created anxiety among genuine cardholders whose cards might be wrongly deemed duplicate.

How to fix:

  • If your card is blocked pending verification, immediately schedule a physical e-KYC validation via Grama One or Karnataka One.
  • Providing electricity bills to show independent dwelling, and obtaining an endorsement from the Village Accountant (VA) will clear the duplicate tag.

Users report high failure rates at Karnataka One, Grama One, and Seva Sindhu service centers while trying to update names, addresses, or add/delete family members.

How to fix:

  • Avoid visiting centers during peak hours (11am - 3pm). The Seva Sindhu portal traffic is notoriously slow during mid-day.
  • If technical failures persist for weeks, approach your Taluk Panchayat office directly, where officials have bypassed access to process backend data corrections.

Reports of dealers charging higher prices, not displaying stock, or providing less than the entitled quantity of food grains.

How to fix:

  • Lodge a formal complaint against the FPS dealer by calling the state toll-free helpline number 1967 or 1800-425-9339.
  • Always insist on receiving the printed receipt generated by the PoS machine. If less ration is given, the printed receipt will serve as hard evidence of the short-changing for the vigilance team.
